content.problemStatement:
Write a program that takes an integer as input and determines whether the number lies in the inclusive range of **10 to 20**.

The program should print:


**"In Range"** if the number is between 10 and 20 (both inclusive).
**"Out of Range"** otherwise.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "In Range" if the integer lies in the inclusive range [10, 20], otherwise print "Out of Range". Each answer is printed on its own line.

sampleTestCases.output:
In Range
Out of Range
Out of Range
sampleTestCases.explanation: 15 lies within [10,20] → In Range; 5 is below the lower bound → Out of Range; 25 is above the upper bound → Out of Range.
10 equals the lower bound, therefore it is considered In Range.
20 equals the upper bound → In Range; 9 is just below the lower bound → Out of Range.

hidden.label: maximum boundary values mixed
hidden.expectedOutput:
Out of Range
In Range
Out of Range

hidden.label: uniform in‑range values
hidden.expectedOutput:
In Range
In Range
In Range
In Range
In Range

hidden.label: boundary edge cases
hidden.expectedOutput:
Out of Range
In Range
In Range
Out of Range

hidden.label: mixed general case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Out of Range
Out of Range
In Range
In Range
In Range
In Range
Out of Range

solution.approach: For each test case we only need to check whether the given integer x satisfies 10 <= x <= 20. This is a direct constant‑time comparison. If the condition holds we output "In Range", otherwise we output "Out of Range". The algorithm processes each test case independently, so the total complexity is linear in the number of test cases.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) overall, O(1) per test case.

code.Python3:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
out = []
idx = 1
for _ in range(T):
    x = int(data[idx])
    idx += 1
    if 10 <= x <= 20:
        out.append('In Range')
    else:
        out.append('Out of Range')
print('\n'.join(out))
